prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of a computer. It does all the thinking and tells the computer what to do, just like how your brain tells your body what to do. It’s a tiny chip inside the computer that works really hard to make sure everything runs smoothly and quickly. Without the CPU, the computer wouldn’t know how to do anything at all!
